item.title: Banks show trading strength, caution on economic risk
item.description: Wall Street banks reported booming trading revenues after a volatile first quarter but sounded caution about the ripple impact of higher oil and geopolitical risk ​on clients.

item.title: Citadel's Ken Griffin says Iran war threatens global recession
item.description: Billionaire investor Ken Griffin warned on Tuesday that the world could face a ​global recession if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for ‌a prolonged period.

item.title: Disney to eliminate 1,000 positions
item.description: Walt Disney new chief executive Josh D'Amaro announced layoffs in an email to ​employees on Tuesday, as he looks to streamline the company's operations.

item.title: War is over for Wall Street, while oil drags down bonds and gold
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East edges towards its eighth week, financial markets are starting to diverge.

item.title: Boeing jet deliveries slow in March due to 737 MAX wiring issue
item.description: Boeing said on Tuesday it delivered 46 jets in March, down from 51 the prior month, as the company repaired ​damaged wiring in about 25 of its best-selling 737 MAX ‌planes.

item.title: Bessent says confident that core inflation is going down, Fed will need to cut rates
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Tuesday said he ​was quite confident that core ‌inflation would continue to go down in the United States despite the ​Iran war, and repeated his ​call for the Federal Reserve to ⁠cut its interest rates.

item.title: 'Blend' countries pay billions extra as access to cheap multilateral loans narrows
item.description: Developing nations are paying tens of billions of dollars extra to fund ‌infrastructure, education and health projects due to inadequate access to affordable loans from multilateral development banks (MDBs), a report showed on Tuesday.

item.title: Amazon launches AI research tool to speed early-stage drug discovery
item.description: Amazon's cloud unit on Tuesday launched Amazon Bio Discovery, an artificial intelligence application designed to speed early-stage drug discovery by ​allowing scientists to run complex computational workflows without writing code.

item.title: IMF warns Middle East war driving up financial stability risks
item.description: The war in the Middle East is elevating ​global financial stability risks through inflationary pressures that could cause funding markets to tighten, potentially straining non-banks, private credit and ‌artificial intelligence borrowers, the International Monetary Fund warned on Tuesday.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee says rate cuts may need to wait until 2027
item.description: Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee on ​Tuesday said interest-rate cuts ‌may need to wait until 2027, depending on how ​long oil prices ​stay high.

item.title: JPMorgan's private credit exposure was at $50 billion, CFO says
item.description: JPMorgan ​Chase's private ‌credit exposure was ​about $50 ​billion, part of ⁠a ​broader exposure ​of $160 billion in non-bank ​financial ​institutions, Chief Financial ‌Officer ⁠Jeremy Barnum told an ​analysts ​call ⁠on Tuesday.

item.title: IMF cuts growth outlook, warns of potential global recession if Iran war worsens
item.description: The International Monetary Fund cut its growth outlook on Tuesday due to Iran war-driven energy price spikes and supply disruptions and warned that the global economy would teeter on ​the brink of recession if the conflict worsens and oil stays above $100 per barrel through 2027.

item.title: Fed nominee Warsh's holdings include SpaceX, Polymarket, crypto and AI
item.description: Holdings in Elon Musk's SpaceX company and predictions platform Polymarket are among dozens of future-oriented assets that Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh lists on a ​newly filed financial disclosure that shows dozens of apparently small bets on a ‌wide array of emerging and almost science fiction-sounding ventures.

item.title: French group Veolia aims $1.2 bln in revenue from data centres, chips by 2030
item.description: French utility Veolia aims to almost double its revenue ​from providing energy and water ‌management solutions to data centres and chipmakers by 2030, it said on Tuesday, ​betting on strong demand from ​both sectors.

item.title: US solar and wind contract prices rose sharply in first quarter
item.description: U.S. solar and wind contract prices rose sharply in the first quarter ​as project developers faced tariffs, labor shortages and ‌permitting challenges amid soaring demand for power from data centers, according to a report published ​on Tuesday.

item.title: IMF expects pace of Japan's interest rate hikes to speed up slightly
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said on Tuesday it expected the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates gradually, but at a ​slightly faster pace than projected six months ago.

item.title: Long Iran war may require painful central bank tightening, IMF chief economist says
item.description: Central banks may need ‌to inflict much more economic pain to control inflation fueled by a long Middle East war than they did to control the spike in prices after the pandemic, the International Monetary Fund's chief economist said.

item.title: Banks press for more capital relief, make plans to use excess capital
item.description: As the largest banks expect to release billions in ​capital with regulatory reform, many are ‌already making plans about how to spend them.

item.title: Global satellite internet companies at a glance
item.description: Amazon.com said on Tuesday it would buy satellite company Globalstar in an $11.57 billion deal, as ​it looks to take on bigger ‌rival SpaceX's Starlink.

item.title: US producer prices increase less than expected in March
item.description: U.S. producer prices increased less than expected in March as the cost of services was unchanged, but surging energy prices ​because of the war with Iran were fanning inflation pressures.

item.title: PepsiCo faces pressure to show Elliott-triggered turnaround is working
item.description: Seven months after activist investor Elliott disclosed a $4 billion stake in PepsiCo, the U.S. ​beverages giant is under pressure to show a turnaround drive including price cuts and brand relaunches can deliver the volume growth investors ‌crave.

item.title: Amazon signs $11.57 billion deal for satellite firm Globalstar to challenge Musk's Starlink
item.description: Amazon.com said on Tuesday it would acquire Globalstar in an $11.57 billion deal, bolstering its fledgling satellite business as it looks to take ​on Elon Musk-led bigger rival Starlink.

item.title: Citi profit jumps 42% as market volatility lifts trading revenue
item.description: Citigroup's first-quarter profit rose 42% as geopolitical tensions fueled market volatility and trading revenue, while strong dealmaking buoyed investment banking fees.

item.title: CarMax posts quarterly loss on goodwill charge, weak used-car demand
item.description: Used car retailer CarMax reported a fourth-quarter loss on Tuesday, hurt by a goodwill impairment charge and ​shrinking margins from used vehicles.

item.title: Grocer Albertsons forecasts annual sales below estimates
item.description: Albertsons forecast its annual sales ​below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, pointing ‌to pressure on demand from fierce competition with bigger rivals rolling out heavier discounts.

item.title: ASML investors bet on 'picks and shovels' of AI revolution
item.description: Investors in , Europe’s most valuable listed company, are betting the chip-equipment maker can raise financial forecasts when it reports ‌first-quarter earnings on Wednesday, as demand for artificial intelligence chips keeps orders for its machines running flat out.

item.title: China orders safety checks on smart vehicle road tests after robotaxi incident
item.description: China has ordered local authorities ​to conduct self-inspections and ‌strengthen safety oversight of road tests involving intelligent ​connected vehicles following ​a recent incident.

item.title: BetMGM cuts 2026 revenue forecast on weak US sports betting
item.description: U.S. online betting operator ​BetMGM cut ‌its 2026 revenue outlook on ​Tuesday, citing ​a soft first-quarter for ⁠its online ​sports betting ​business.

item.title: EV maker Lucid names Silvio Napoli as CEO, shares rise
item.description: Lucid on Tuesday ‌named Silvio Napoli as the company's ​next chief ​executive officer, as it ⁠looks to ramp ​up production and ​sales of its electric vehicles amid a ​highly competitive EV ​environment.

item.title: Bond investors target steeper US yield curve on bets for slower growth, more debt issuance
item.description: Bond investors are doubling down on so-called "curve steepener" trades that are bullish on short-dated U.S. Treasuries but bearish on the long end, reflecting expectations that the Federal Reserve will eventually resume cutting interest rates.
